text: Yes, Sir.

text: Yeah, I sort of thought so myself.
en: Yeah, I sort of thought so myself.

text: Better watch out for that kind, Zackie. You know what they call 'em, don't ya?
en: Better watch out for that kind, Zackie. You know what they call 'em, don't ya?

text: Yeah, I know.
en: Yeah, I know.

text: Back east in Newport, Rhode Island, they call 'em the Fall River Debs. In Pensacola, the Mobile Debs. In Norfolk --
en: Back east in Newport, Rhode Island, they call 'em the Fall River Debs. In Pensacola, the Mobile Debs. In Norfolk --

text: That what she was... a Norfolk Deb?
en: That what she was... a Norfolk Deb?

text: Who?  Aw shit, Zackie, let's not get off on your mother again, please.
en: Who?  Aw shit, Zackie, let's not get off on your mother again, please.

text: What if I want to talk about her, pard? What then? You know, that's all I've ever heard from you, since I was a kid... you never want to talk about that, man, and it's important.
en: What if I want to talk about her, pard? What then? You know, that's all I've ever heard from you, since I was a kid... you never want to talk about that, man, and it's important.

text: There's nothing to talk about. Two goddamn times I made it with your old lady. We barely even talked.
en: There's nothing to talk about. Two goddamn times I made it with your old lady. We barely even talked.

text: I wrote. Not every week...
en: I wrote. Not every week...

text: She said you told her in every letter how much you loved her, how you wanted to marry her, have children with her...
en: She said you told her in every letter how much you loved her, how you wanted to marry her, have children with her...

text: I never said any of that!
en: I never said any of that!

text: I found them, pard, and read them myself, right after she did it!
en: I found them, pard, and read them myself, right after she did it!

text: Okay, I wrote those things... and yeah, I had big thoughts of getting together with your mom... but when she hit me with being pregnant, I saw who she was. I'd had quiff lay that shit on me before!
en: Okay, I wrote those things... and yeah, I had big thoughts of getting together with your mom... but when she hit me with being pregnant, I saw who she was. I'd had quiff lay that shit on me before!

text: What did you call her? What did I hear you call her, you son of a bitch?
en: What did you call her? What did I hear you call her, you son of a bitch?

text: Nothing. Go back to the show, Paula.
en: Nothing. Go back to the show, Paula.

text: I've seen all that a hundred times.
en: I've seen all that a hundred times.

text: Hey, will you just leave me alone?
en: Hey, will you just leave me alone?

text: Zack, I don't know if I want to do that...
en: Zack, I don't know if I want to do that...

text: When I'm good and ready.
en: When I'm good and ready.

text: I haven't made up my mind.
en: I haven't made up my mind.

text: What do you mean?
en: What do you mean?

text: You know what I mean. Would you... let yourself get pregnant?
en: You know what I mean. Would you... let yourself get pregnant?

text: No way... Would you?
en: No way... Would you?

text: I never used to think I'd do something like that, but now I'm not so sure. You ask me, nine weeks just ain't long enough to get a guy to fall in love with you.
en: I never used to think I'd do something like that, but now I'm not so sure. You ask me, nine weeks just ain't long enough to get a guy to fall in love with you.

text: That don't justify trying to trap a boy by getting pregnant, Lynette! Nothing justifies that. I can't believe you're even thinking like that. I mean, that's really backward.
en: That don't justify trying to trap a boy by getting pregnant, Lynette! Nothing justifies that. I can't believe you're even thinking like that. I mean, that's really backward.

text: No more backward, if you ask me, than the way these hotshot assholes fuck us, then ditch us.  Don't you ever feel used, Paula? Don't you ever feel like if this is all you get for your trouble then the sonofabitch ought to be paying for it...?
en: No more backward, if you ask me, than the way these hotshot assholes fuck us, then ditch us.  Don't you ever feel used, Paula? Don't you ever feel like if this is all you get for your trouble then the sonofabitch ought to be paying for it...?

text: No. I never feel like that.
en: No. I never feel like that.

text: I do.
en: I do.

text: Are those for me?
en: Are those for me?

text: No, they're for your mom.
en: No, they're for your mom.

text: I don't mean anything by it.
en: I don't mean anything by it.

text: No, it's okay. It was a great free meal. Everybody was so uptight I felt sorry for you.
en: No, it's okay. It was a great free meal. Everybody was so uptight I felt sorry for you.

text: That's okay. I'm used to it.
en: That's okay. I'm used to it.

text: Yeah, then if I get jets, it's on to Beeville, Texas.
en: Yeah, then if I get jets, it's on to Beeville, Texas.

text: No. Is that what you want?
en: No. Is that what you want?

text: Some day. When I'm sure I can do a better job of it than my folks.
en: Some day. When I'm sure I can do a better job of it than my folks.

text: What would you do differently?
en: What would you do differently?

text: For a start, I wouldn't marry a man I wasn't in love with.
en: For a start, I wouldn't marry a man I wasn't in love with.

text: Why'd your mom marry that guy if she didn't love him?
en: Why'd your mom marry that guy if she didn't love him?

text: Because my real father wouldn't marry her.
en: Because my real father wouldn't marry her.

text: Your real father?
en: Your real father?

text: Twenty-two years ago.
en: Twenty-two years ago.

text: No wonder he was looking at me like that.
en: No wonder he was looking at me like that.

text: I'll try, but this week we go into survival training, so I can't make any promises.  Well, thanks again for dinner. Thank your mom again for me, will you?
en: I'll try, but this week we go into survival training, so I can't make any promises.  Well, thanks again for dinner. Thank your mom again for me, will you?

text: Sure.  Zack, I hope you know I didn't have to show you that picture.
en: Sure.  Zack, I hope you know I didn't have to show you that picture.

text: I know that.
en: I know that.

text: You'd better get smart, man. It's time to walk away.
en: You'd better get smart, man. It's time to walk away.

text: What? You've gotta be kidding!
en: What? You've gotta be kidding!

text: Remember what Foley said? His little warning? Those are the girls he was talking about. They're out to marry us any way they can.
en: Remember what Foley said? His little warning? Those are the girls he was talking about. They're out to marry us any way they can.

text: I don't believe that. They're just having a good time, same as us.
en: I don't believe that. They're just having a good time, same as us.

text: That's what they want you to think, but I saw where she lived, what is she's trying to get away from.  Just take my word for it, pard. Break it off now. Do it this week.
en: That's what they want you to think, but I saw where she lived, what is she's trying to get away from.  Just take my word for it, pard. Break it off now. Do it this week.

text: Ashamed? No -- I love you, Lynette. I mean that. After I leave them, I'll meet you at the motel, okay?
en: Ashamed? No -- I love you, Lynette. I mean that. After I leave them, I'll meet you at the motel, okay?

text: If you won't take me to dinner with your parents, I won't meet you at the motel.
en: If you won't take me to dinner with your parents, I won't meet you at the motel.

text: Lynette, I told you already, it won't work.
en: Lynette, I told you already, it won't work.

text: Then, I'll see you around.
en: Then, I'll see you around.

text: No problem, but who's Susan?
en: No problem, but who's Susan?

text: My girl back home. We're supposed to get married after I get my wings. She was Tommy's girl. They were engaged to be married before he died.  I should've told you about her. I don't know why I didn't, except I didn't want you to think I was a shit for making it with Lynette.
en: My girl back home. We're supposed to get married after I get my wings. She was Tommy's girl. They were engaged to be married before he died.  I should've told you about her. I don't know why I didn't, except I didn't want you to think I was a shit for making it with Lynette.

text: I'm not your folks, man. You love this... Susan?
en: I'm not your folks, man. You love this... Susan?

text: She's the sweetest person I've ever known. Loves kids. Works with handicapped kids every afternoon at the church. Everybody loves her.
en: She's the sweetest person I've ever known. Loves kids. Works with handicapped kids every afternoon at the church. Everybody loves her.

text: I didn't ask you all that, Sweet Pea. I asked if you loved her.
en: I didn't ask you all that, Sweet Pea. I asked if you loved her.

text: Listen, I'm not going to go to that little reunion party. I'm meeting Lynette at the motel.  Best head in fifty-two states. After three days of survival training, how could I resist?
en: Listen, I'm not going to go to that little reunion party. I'm meeting Lynette at the motel.  Best head in fifty-two states. After three days of survival training, how could I resist?

text: Lynette told me it really tore her up when you didn't call this week.
en: Lynette told me it really tore her up when you didn't call this week.

text: Just trying to have fun. That fucking prison is really starting to get to me. C'mon, Seeger. Gimme a push.  Fuck you guys! I'll do it myself!
en: Just trying to have fun. That fucking prison is really starting to get to me. C'mon, Seeger. Gimme a push.  Fuck you guys! I'll do it myself!

text: I'm a little late, that's all.
en: I'm a little late, that's all.

text: How late?
en: How late?

text: What difference does it make? If anything was to happen, which I'm sure it isn't, it would be my responsibility.
en: What difference does it make? If anything was to happen, which I'm sure it isn't, it would be my responsibility.

text: Exactly how late are you, Lynette?
en: Exactly how late are you, Lynette?

text: What do you care? Suppose I was pregnant. Just suppose it. You don't think I'd try to make you do anything you don't want to, do you?
en: What do you care? Suppose I was pregnant. Just suppose it. You don't think I'd try to make you do anything you don't want to, do you?

text: No. But that's not the only issue here, sweetheart. There's a lot more to it than that.
en: No. But that's not the only issue here, sweetheart. There's a lot more to it than that.

text: My responsibility as its father, for one. I mean, if I've made you pregnant, I'd want to... do the right thing.
en: My responsibility as its father, for one. I mean, if I've made you pregnant, I'd want to... do the right thing.

text: Let's just wait and see what happens.
en: Let's just wait and see what happens.

text: But it can't wait.
en: But it can't wait.

text: No, but she's gotta be at least a month late.
en: No, but she's gotta be at least a month late.

text: Doesn't mean shit. Get her to a doctor. You can't do anything until you hear what he says. Make the appointment yourself.
en: Doesn't mean shit. Get her to a doctor. You can't do anything until you hear what he says. Make the appointment yourself.

text: Fuck the record. Now you listen to me and do exactly what I tell you.  Start back ten yards and take off from here. Not here... or there... but right here!  No excuses, Seeger! You are going to plant those legs here and then you're going to yank yourself over that wall because you have to! You want jets? Then do it, goddamnit!
en: Fuck the record. Now you listen to me and do exactly what I tell you.  Start back ten yards and take off from here. Not here... or there... but right here!  No excuses, Seeger! You are going to plant those legs here and then you're going to yank yourself over that wall because you have to! You want jets? Then do it, goddamnit!

text: But she expects you to marry her?
en: But she expects you to marry her?

text: She said it was up to me. If I don't, she'll go off and have the baby on her own somewhere.
en: She said it was up to me. If I don't, she'll go off and have the baby on her own somewhere.

text: I can't let her go off and have the kid by herself and not do anything. If it's my kid, too, then I've got a responsibility, don't I?
en: I can't let her go off and have the kid by herself and not do anything. If it's my kid, too, then I've got a responsibility, don't I?

text: Not if she won't even talk about an abortion.
en: Not if she won't even talk about an abortion.

text: But it would still be my kid. That's the point.
en: But it would still be my kid. That's the point.

text: Do you know that for sure?
en: Do you know that for sure?

text: It's mine.
en: It's mine.

text: No matter how it happened, if she goes ahead and has it" Zack, there'll be a child in the world that's mine -- and I couldn't go through life knowing that and not knowing its name or where it lived.
en: No matter how it happened, if she goes ahead and has it" Zack, there'll be a child in the world that's mine -- and I couldn't go through life knowing that and not knowing its name or where it lived.

text: Jesus Christ, Sid! Is everything your responsibility?
en: Jesus Christ, Sid! Is everything your responsibility?

text: I don't know... I felt like... like I was suffocating... Christ, Zack... I was so scared... so godddamn scared...
en: I don't know... I felt like... like I was suffocating... Christ, Zack... I was so scared... so godddamn scared...

text: It doesn't matter what he's going through. That's the whole purpose of this zoo. What matters is he freaked out for some reason at twenty-five thousand feet and that can't ever happen again.
en: It doesn't matter what he's going through. That's the whole purpose of this zoo. What matters is he freaked out for some reason at twenty-five thousand feet and that can't ever happen again.

text: But you don't understand. There's this girl he's gotten pregnant and she's putting him through hell, sir.
en: But you don't understand. There's this girl he's gotten pregnant and she's putting him through hell, sir.

text: Just like that it's all over? With less than two weeks to go, you're out?
en: Just like that it's all over? With less than two weeks to go, you're out?

text: Zack, don't!
en: Zack, don't!

text: Stop eyeballing me, Mayo, or you're out!
en: Stop eyeballing me, Mayo, or you're out!

text: I don't get it! He's the best candidate in our class! Ask anyone! The best student! The best leader! The best friend to everybody! Couldn't you bend your goddamn standards just a little?
en: I don't get it! He's the best candidate in our class! Ask anyone! The best student! The best leader! The best friend to everybody! Couldn't you bend your goddamn standards just a little?

text: Zack, it wasn't him! He didn't ask me to D.O.R. I came to him on my own.  I'm glad it's over, Zack. I really mean that. He was right. I wasn't doing this for me.
en: Zack, it wasn't him! He didn't ask me to D.O.R. I came to him on my own.  I'm glad it's over, Zack. I really mean that. He was right. I wasn't doing this for me.

text: So?
en: So?

text: Paula, he DORed and nobody's seen him.
en: Paula, he DORed and nobody's seen him.

text: Why'd he do it?
en: Why'd he do it?

text: Hey! You know goddamn well what happened so let's not play any games, okay?
en: Hey! You know goddamn well what happened so let's not play any games, okay?

text: I'm not playing any games! Go look at Lynette's!
en: I'm not playing any games! Go look at Lynette's!

text: I don't know where that is.
en: I don't know where that is.

text: What're you doing out of uniform, Sid? You don't want to get in trouble.
en: What're you doing out of uniform, Sid? You don't want to get in trouble.

text: Forget that. Come on. Got a little surprise...
en: Forget that. Come on. Got a little surprise...

text: I can't go like this. Can't you wait a few minutes 'til I'm ready?
en: I can't go like this. Can't you wait a few minutes 'til I'm ready?

text: No way. I'm so happy I'm about to bust. Here, honey. This is for you. It cost me my whole savings, but I said what the fuck.
en: No way. I'm so happy I'm about to bust. Here, honey. This is for you. It cost me my whole savings, but I said what the fuck.

text: That's right. Let's get married, Lynette. Let's find a justice of the peace and just do it!
en: That's right. Let's get married, Lynette. Let's find a justice of the peace and just do it!

text: We're not gonna be stationed anywhere, baby. I DORed.
en: We're not gonna be stationed anywhere, baby. I DORed.

text: You what?
en: You what?

text: I had to, baby... I'm no aviator. I was faking it, like I was with everything else in my life... up 'til right now.
en: I had to, baby... I'm no aviator. I was faking it, like I was with everything else in my life... up 'til right now.

text: But... but what would we do? Where would we go?
en: But... but what would we do? Where would we go?

text: Oklahoma. I can get my old job back at JC Penney's. In a couple of years, I'll be floor manager. Oh, you're gonna love Oklahoma, Lynette. You and mama'll get along just great. Of course, money will be a little tight for a while, but we'll make it.
en: Oklahoma. I can get my old job back at JC Penney's. In a couple of years, I'll be floor manager. Oh, you're gonna love Oklahoma, Lynette. You and mama'll get along just great. Of course, money will be a little tight for a while, but we'll make it.

text: Sid, there's no baby.
en: Sid, there's no baby.

text: What?
en: What?

text: I'm not pregnant. I got my period this morning. There's no baby, Sid.
en: I'm not pregnant. I got my period this morning. There's no baby, Sid.

text: Well, I'll be goddamned.
en: Well, I'll be goddamned.

text: Already come and gone. Can you believe it? He DORed in the twelfth week. How can you win?
en: Already come and gone. Can you believe it? He DORed in the twelfth week. How can you win?

text: That there isn't one, as of today. I had my period.  I couldn't believe it. He still wanted to marry me.
en: That there isn't one, as of today. I had my period.  I couldn't believe it. He still wanted to marry me.

text: And you turned him down??
en: And you turned him down??

text: Of course. I don't want no Okie from Muscogee. I can get that right here in Port Angeles.
en: Of course. I don't want no Okie from Muscogee. I can get that right here in Port Angeles.

text: Of course there was a baby. I'd never lie about something like that. Would I, Paula?
en: Of course there was a baby. I'd never lie about something like that. Would I, Paula?

text: You're no better than me, Paula! You're just the same!
en: You're no better than me, Paula! You're just the same!

text: No! That's not true!
en: No! That's not true!

text: Why?
en: Why?

text: Because he's my friend, too.
en: Because he's my friend, too.

text: Come on, Paula! You were looking for a ticket out of here and you didn't care who it was, any more than you cared with the last class of candidates you and Lynette fucked your way through, looking for a husband! Or the class before that!
en: Come on, Paula! You were looking for a ticket out of here and you didn't care who it was, any more than you cared with the last class of candidates you and Lynette fucked your way through, looking for a husband! Or the class before that!

text: Yeah. You got the whole story just right.
en: Yeah. You got the whole story just right.

text: Beware of the Puget Debs -- and we all laughed, especially him.
en: Beware of the Puget Debs -- and we all laughed, especially him.

text: I'm not a Puget Deb. I hate that goddamn term!
en: I'm not a Puget Deb. I hate that goddamn term!

text: I bet you do!
en: I bet you do!

text: However you got it figured, I didn't kill Sid and Lynette didn't kill him! He killed himself!
en: However you got it figured, I didn't kill Sid and Lynette didn't kill him! He killed himself!

text: That's brilliant.
en: That's brilliant.

text: Maybe not, but it is the truth. And Zack, you didn't kill him either.
en: Maybe not, but it is the truth. And Zack, you didn't kill him either.

text: Sir, this officer candidate requests permission to speak to you in private.
en: Sir, this officer candidate requests permission to speak to you in private.

text: I'm busy, Mayo. It'll have to wait.
en: I'm busy, Mayo. It'll have to wait.

text: It's important, sir!
en: It's important, sir!

text: Mayo, you didn't hear me -- I said I I'm busy! And so are you! Go get cleaned up!
en: Mayo, you didn't hear me -- I said I I'm busy! And so are you! Go get cleaned up!

text: Aw screw it...
en: Aw screw it...

text: Yes, sir!
en: Yes, sir!

text: Get on your feet and find out how good, sir.
en: Get on your feet and find out how good, sir.

text: Naw. That's over with.
en: Naw. That's over with.

text: I'll never forget you as long as I live, Sergeant.
en: I'll never forget you as long as I live, Sergeant.

text: I know.
en: I know.

text: Well, goodbye.
en: Well, goodbye.

text: Yeah. See you in the fleet, Sarge. And thank you.
en: Yeah. See you in the fleet, Sarge. And thank you.
